leads.Lead_Details__c: Leander ISD Bond 2023-funded replacement of natural grass with artificial turf at high school baseball and softball fields at five campuses: Cedar Park HS, Leander HS, Rouse HS, Vandegrift HS, and Vista Ridge HS. Budget: $27,601,380. Contractor: Hellas Construction. Status: all five schools completed as of March 2025. Stated benefit: allows practice in various weather conditions without damaging the competition surface. [Source: SRC-001 | Found at: https://www.leanderisd.org/bond_athleticsprojects/]
leads.Title: Chief Operations Officer (Facilities)

leads.Lead_Details__c: Leander ISD Board of Trustees approved reallocation of 2023 Bond funds (~$1.6 million) to replace end-of-life artificial turf at Bible Stadium (A.C. Bible Jr. Memorial Stadium, football/soccer stadium) and Monroe Stadium (Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium, football/soccer stadium). Existing turf presents safety concerns for student-athletes; replacement planned for summer 2026 to avoid disrupting athletic seasons. Funding reallocated from surplus savings in the 2023 Bond's baseball/softball turf field projects at five high schools (completed by March 2025 via contractor Hellas Construction). [Source: SRC-001, SRC-002 | Found at: https://www.leanderisd.org/bond_athleticsprojects/ ; https://news.leanderisd.org/board-briefs-jan-22-2026/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Bible Stadium (A.C. Bible Jr. Memorial Stadium), 3301 S Bagdad Rd, Leander, TX 78613; Monroe Stadium (Ed W. Monroe Memorial Stadium), 9500 McNeil Dr, Austin, TX 78750
leads.Title: Chief Operations Officer (Facilities)

leads.Lead_Details__c: Leander ISD Bond 2023 project to replace natural grass with artificial turf at the Running Brushy MS competition field. Budget: $1,186,500. Timeline: Summer 2024 - August 2025 (completed). Note: RBMS is described as the only middle school campus in the central/north part of the district with bleachers and lights, indicating a competition-level multipurpose field; specific sport (football/soccer) not explicitly named on source page, so sport needs validation. [Source: SRC-001 | Found at: https://www.leanderisd.org/bond_athleticsprojects/]
leads.Title: Chief Operations Officer (Facilities)

leads.Lead_Details__c: Round Rock ISD 2024 Bond (Proposition D, $65.9M / $104M with interest) includes turf replacement of five middle school competition football/soccer fields: Chisholm Trail, Hernandez, Hopewell, Pearson Ranch, and Walsh middle schools. Turf replacements scheduled to begin fall 2026 and conclude late 2027. Related lighting work for the same competition football and soccer fields (Canyon Vista, Chisholm Trail, Pearson Ranch, Walsh middle schools) is in procurement. Bond program managed by HPM on behalf of the district. Sport confirmed: football/soccer explicitly stated. [Source: SRC-003, SRC-004 | Found at: https://communityimpact.com/austin/round-rock/education/2026/05/26/round-rock-isd-heads-into-summer-of-bond-funded-construction/]
leads.Title: Director of Construction and Facilities Bond

leads.Lead_Details__c: Round Rock ISD 2024 Bond (Proposition D) includes artificial turf conversion for baseball and softball competition fields at multiple high schools. McNeil High School and Stony Point High School are specifically named for baseball/softball turf conversion (McNeil: turf replacement for baseball and softball facilities, plus bleacher/press box improvements; Stony Point: baseball/softball turf conversion plus scoreboard replacement, field structure improvements, track replacement, press box improvements). Cedar Ridge and Westwood high schools have related baseball/softball lighting work in procurement as part of the same athletic facilities program. Bond program managed by HPM on behalf of the district. Sport confirmed: baseball/softball explicitly stated. [Source: SRC-003, SRC-004 | Found at: https://communityimpact.com/austin/round-rock/education/2025/04/22/round-rock-isd-lays-out-first-year-plans-for-2024-bond-projects/]
leads.Title: Director of Construction and Facilities Bond

leads.Lead_Details__c: RFP 17-082501AS: Middle School Turf and Track Project. Scope: track repair and turf field installation at 6 middle schools (Simon, Chapa, McCormick, Dahlstrom, Barton, Wallace Middle Schools), funded by 2025 bond, approx. $17.34M total ($2.89M/school avg), vendor Hellas Construction. Bid due 09/24/2025, pre-bid meeting 09/09/2025. NOTE: sport needs validation — source describes 'turf fields' and running tracks at middle school athletic complexes but does not explicitly name football, soccer, baseball, or softball. [Source: SRC-005 | Found at: https://texasbids.net/government-agencies/hays/hays-consolidated-independent-school-district-156241/14395896-middle-school-turf-and-track-project-rfp.html]
leads.Project_Address__c: Simon MS: 3829 FM 150, Kyle, TX 78640; Chapa MS: 3311 Dacy Ln., Kyle, TX 78640; McCormick MS: 5700 Dacy Ln., Kyle, TX 78640; Dahlstrom MS: 3600 FM 967, Buda, TX 78610; Barton MS: 4950 Jack C. Hays Trl., Buda, TX 78610; Wallace MS: 1500 W. Center St., Kyle, TX 78640
leads.Title: Senior Buyer

leads.Lead_Details__c: Hays CISD 2025 Bond (Prop A, approved by voters 60.02% on 5/3/2025) is funding an artificial turf install at Simon Middle School as part of a campus expansion to 1,200 students. Trustees approved a combined Guaranteed Maximum Price of $26,929,942 (Simon MS + Hemphill Elementary) to Bartlett Cocke Construction Company on 5/19/2025. Construction started June 2025 with expected completion June 2026. Sport not explicitly specified in public reporting (likely multipurpose football/soccer practice field) — needs validation. [Source: SRC-006 | Found at: https://communityimpact.com/austin/san-marcos-buda-kyle/education/2025/05/20/hays-cisd-bond-projects-at-middle-school-elementaries-make-early-progress/]
leads.Title: Executive Director of Facilities, Construction and Bond Programs

leads.Lead_Details__c: Hays CISD 2025 Bond Proposition A (passed 60.02% on 5/3/2025) allocated $6.7 million for artificial turf installation at Barton, Chapa, and Dahlstrom middle schools, part of a districtwide effort to install artificial turf at all middle school athletic fields to reduce water use. Sport/field type at each individual campus (football/soccer multipurpose vs. baseball/softball) is not specified in public reporting and needs validation. [Source: SRC-006 | Found at: https://communityimpact.com/austin/san-marcos-buda-kyle/education/2025/01/17/latest-updates-on-hays-cisds-986-million-5-proposition-bond/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Barton Middle School, 4950 Jack C. Hays Trail, Buda, TX 78610; Chapa Middle School, 3311 Dacy Lane, Kyle, TX 78640; Dahlstrom Middle School, 3600 FM 967, Buda, TX 78610
leads.Title: Executive Director of Facilities, Construction and Bond Programs

leads.Lead_Details__c: Hays CISD 2025 Bond Proposition B (passed 56.82% on 5/3/2025, $396.5M/$372.5M base) funds new High School #4 in Buda, opening 2029-30, designed for 2,800 students, and explicitly includes softball, baseball, and tennis facilities. TDLR registration (TABS2026003500) lists the project at an estimated $355.0M / 659,313 sq ft, construction start 12/16/2025, expected completion 1/29/2029. Turf vs. natural grass for the baseball/softball fields is not confirmed in public documents and needs validation. Note: a related Proposition C ($6M stadium expansion for this same campus) FAILED at the ballot (47.78% for) and has no active funding as of this research. [Source: SRC-006 | Found at: https://www.kut.org/education/2025-04-21/hays-cisd-has-a-nearly-1-billion-bond-on-the-may-3-ballot-heres-whats-in-it]
leads.Project_Address__c: 14104 S Turnersville Road, Buda, TX 78610
leads.Title: Executive Director of Facilities, Construction and Bond Programs

leads.Lead_Details__c: Austin ISD 2022 Bond Program - High School Athletic Field Upgrades: All 12 comprehensive high schools (Akins ECHS, Anderson HS, Austin HS, Bowie HS, Crockett ECHS, Eastside ECHS, LBJ ECHS, LASA, McCallum HS, Navarro ECHS, Northeast ECHS/International HS, Travis ECHS) to receive artificial turf and athletic field improvements (lighting). Fields serve as practice facilities for high school football/soccer athletics plus middle school games, track meets, and PE. Design contracts completed Oct 2023. 8 campuses under construction as of mid-2025; majority of fields expected complete by end of 2025, a few in early 2026; Anderson HS construction anticipated to begin March 2026; Crockett ECHS and Travis ECHS to be constructed with modernization projects (dates TBD). Sport needs validation per campus. [Source: SRC-008 | Found at: https://bond.austinschools.org/bond-projects/high-school-athletic-turf-and-fields]
leads.Project_Address__c: Austin ISD comprehensive high schools (12 campuses) - Austin, TX
leads.Projected_Start_Date__c: March 2026 (Anderson HS); others under construction as of mid-2025
leads.Title: Coordinator Contract Management Construction, Bond Procurement Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Austin ISD 2022 Bond Program - Yellow Jacket Stadium: Central athletic facility upgrade including artificial turf installation, track resurfacing, press box, fieldhouse, scoreboard, lighting, seating, concessions, and restroom upgrades for football/soccer facilities. Budget: $6,987,758. Design began fall 2023 (project team: Vanir STV program manager; Fuse Architecture Studio architecture/engineering). [Source: SRC-008 | Found at: https://bond.austinschools.org/bond-projects/central-athletic-facilities]
leads.Project_Address__c: 3101 Hargrave Street, Austin, TX
leads.Title: Coordinator Contract Management Construction, Bond Procurement Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Austin ISD 2022 Bond Program - Nelson Field (baseball): Central athletic facility upgrade including grandstand and field house renovations, baseball field artificial turf, new seating and lighting. Budget: $25,000,000. Design began fall 2023 (project team: Vanir STV program manager; Stantec/Cadence McShane architecture & construction). [Source: SRC-008 | Found at: https://bond.austinschools.org/bond-projects/central-athletic-facilities]
leads.Project_Address__c: 7104 Berkman Drive, Austin, TX
leads.Title: Coordinator Contract Management Construction, Bond Procurement Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Austin ISD 2022 Bond Program - Burger Sports Complex (baseball): Central athletic facility upgrade including artificial turf installation, lighting, and seating upgrades for baseball field. Budget: $4,767,000. Design began fall 2023. [Source: SRC-008 | Found at: https://bond.austinschools.org/bond-projects/central-athletic-facilities]
leads.Project_Address__c: 3200 Jones Road, Austin, TX
leads.Title: Coordinator Contract Management Construction, Bond Procurement Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Austin ISD 2022 Bond Program - Noack Sports Complex (softball): Central athletic facility upgrade including artificial turf installation, lighting, and seating for softball field. Budget: $2,667,000. Design began fall 2023. [Source: SRC-008 | Found at: https://bond.austinschools.org/bond-projects/central-athletic-facilities]
leads.Project_Address__c: 5300 Crainway Drive, Austin, TX
leads.Title: Coordinator Contract Management Construction, Bond Procurement Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Awarded contract: RFQ 26-008JO Pflugerville High School Baseball and Softball Field Turf Installation. Design-build RFQ for turnkey baseball/softball turf field installation at Pflugerville High School; estimated project budget $2,500,000. Awarded to Paragon Sports Constructors, LLC (board approval referenced 1/15/2026). [Source: SRC-009 | Found at: https://www.pfisd.net/about-us/departments/procurement-and-auxiliary-services/information-for-vendors/current-bid-results]
leads.Project_Address__c: 1301 W Pecan St, Pflugerville, TX 78660
leads.Title: Director of Procurement & Auxiliary Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Awarded contract: CSP 26-024JO Hendrickson High School and Weiss High School Baseball and Softball Turf Conversion. Bid tabulation shows evaluated vendors FieldTurf USA DBA Tarkett Sports, G Hyatt Construction, Hellas Construction Inc, Legacy Sports Construction, Paragon Sports Constructors, Rgroup Inc DBA United Turf Track; awarded to Hellas Construction, Inc. Board agenda 6/18/2026, award/renewal window through 11/30/2026. [Source: SRC-009 | Found at: https://www.pfisd.net/about-us/departments/procurement-and-auxiliary-services/information-for-vendors/current-bid-results]
leads.Project_Address__c: Hendrickson High School, 19201 Colorado Sand Dr, Pflugerville, TX 78660; Weiss High School, 5201 Wolf Pack Dr, Pflugerville, TX 78660
leads.Title: Executive Director of Facilities & Support Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: Awarded contract: RFP 25-018JO Pflugerville High School and Connally High School Sub-Varsity Football Field Turf Replacement. Football field turf replacement for sub-varsity stadium fields at Pflugerville HS and Connally HS. Proposals accepted until Jan 7, 2025; awarded to Paragon Sports per award letter dated Feb 21, 2025 (addressed to Lee Penland, Paragon Sports). [Source: SRC-009 | Found at: https://www.pfisd.net/about-us/departments/procurement-and-auxiliary-services/information-for-vendors/current-bid-results]
leads.Project_Address__c: Pflugerville High School, 1301 W Pecan St, Pflugerville, TX 78660; John B Connally High School, 13212 N Lamar Blvd, Austin, TX 78753
leads.Title: Executive Director of Facilities & Support Services

leads.Lead_Details__c: New Georgetown High School (#4) construction project, approx. 530,000 SF, includes new football field, baseball field & softball field (plus tennis courts and school facilities/infrastructure/parking). Solicited via Bid Package 1 (Civil/Structural, due 02/27/25) and Bid Package 2 (due 03/20/25 and 04/08/25), Solicitation No. 14044197. Turf material/surface type not specified in available listing — needs validation. Sport confirmed (football, baseball, softball fields explicitly named); bid due dates are in the past so award status should be verified directly with GISD Purchasing. [Source: SRC-010 | Found at: https://texasbids.net/government-agencies/williamson/georgetown-isd-157330/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Georgetown High School, DB Woods Rd (at Cedar Breaks Road), Georgetown, TX

leads.Lead_Details__c: Hutto ISD is replacing/upgrading the field turf at Hutto Memorial Stadium (football stadium, home of Hutto High School football), with an adjusted design realigning the midfield logo with the center of the home stands. Work was underway as of June 2026 and targeted for completion before the August 28, 2026 football home opener vs. Harker Heights. Square footage, product specifications, and contractor not stated on source page. [Source: SRC-011 | Found at: https://www.hipponation.org/hippo-nation-news-post/~board/district-news-hutto-independent-school-district-12/post/memorial-stadium-turf-among-several-upgrades-underway-across-hutto-isd-this-summer]
leads.Project_Address__c: 200 College Street, Hutto, TX 78634 (Hutto Memorial Stadium)
leads.Title: Director of Capital Improvement & Facilities Planning

leads.Lead_Details__c: San Marcos CISD 2023 Bond Proposition C ($984,563, approved by voters May 2023) funded artificial turf replacement at San Marcos High School's Rattler Stadium (football/multipurpose stadium), plus the track practice field, baseball field, and softball field. Board of Trustees awarded the construction contract to Paragon Sports Constructors in May 2023; installed system was Shaw Sports Turf 'Game On' hybrid turf with Brock SP 17XL pad and sand/rubber/GeoFill infill. Project came in under the not-to-exceed budget of $984,563 and was expedited to complete before football season. [Source: SRC-012 | Found at: https://communityimpact.com/austin/san-marcos-buda-kyle/education/2023/05/16/san-marcos-cisd-approves-construction-contract-for-field-turf-replacement-at-rattler-stadium/]
leads.Project_Address__c: 2602 Rattler Road, San Marcos, TX (Rattler Stadium / San Marcos High School)
leads.Title: Director of State and Federal Programs

leads.Lead_Details__c: Del Valle ISD High School #2 (North Del Valle HS), a new $240M comprehensive high school under construction in the Whisper Valley neighborhood (southeast Travis County / Del Valle, TX area), funded by the district's $300 million 2022 bond program. District and contractor sources describe the athletic scope as 'athletic field events, football, baseball, and softball fields' plus a field house and open-air multipurpose building. Del Valle ISD's own bond-update page states these baseball/softball fields will be 'equitable to those at Del Valle High School,' which have confirmed synthetic turf (Shaw Sports Turf) from a prior 2019 bond project — however, no explicit artificial/synthetic turf specification was found on the district's own site for HS#2, so turf material and exact field count/type need direct validation with the district or CM-at-risk contractor (American Constructors). Total project size approximately 473,338 sq ft (main building + field house + concession + baseball/softball facilities). Construction manager-at-risk: American Constructors; Architect: Pfluger Architects. [Source: SRC-013 | Found at: https://www.dvisd.net/departments/construction-planning/bond-updates/high-school-2-updates]
leads.Project_Address__c: Whisper Valley neighborhood, Del Valle ISD (northern portion of district), Del Valle/Manor, TX area; general district office at 5301 Ross Rd, Del Valle, TX 78617

leads.Lead_Details__c: Lake Travis ISD 2023 bond ($143M) funded Cavalier Stadium renovation at Lake Travis High School: GMP of $48.31 million approved July 15, 2025 with general contractor White Construction. Scope includes new artificial turf for the baseball stadium and softball stadium, new turf for the track stadium (plus seating and fencing), and new multipurpose turf fields at the stadium complex. Also includes women's field house, expanded seating (7,400 to 12,000), press box, and concession/restroom renovations. Construction expected to take ~13 months with completion around October 2026. [Source: SRC-014 | Found at: https://www.ltisdschools.org/bond/bond-2023]
leads.Project_Address__c: Lake Travis High School, 3324 RR 620 S, Austin, TX 78738
leads.Title: Director of Purchasing

leads.Lead_Details__c: Lake Travis ISD 2023 bond ($143M) funding new Highland Lakes High School (High School #2), a 150-acre campus off SH 71 on Reimers-Peacock Road. Athletic facilities include a football stadium, baseball field, softball field, and a track & field/soccer stadium, plus practice fields and tennis courts. Bond page states the campus includes 'dedicated facilities for football/soccer, baseball, softball, and tennis competition, plus a track/auxiliary stadium.' Construction manager: American Constructors. Construction began January 26, 2026; substantial completion expected Q1 2029; school opening August 2029. Total project cost $219 million. Note: turf vs. natural grass surface for the competition stadium/fields was not explicitly confirmed on official pages (bond page separately references 'grass practice fields' for practice use only) — sport is confirmed (football/soccer, baseball, softball) but turf specification for the competition fields should be validated directly with the district. [Source: SRC-014 | Found at: https://www.ltisdschools.org/bond/hs2]
leads.Project_Address__c: Reimers-Peacock Road off SH 71, Austin, TX area
leads.Title: Director of Purchasing

leads.Lead_Details__c: Historical/awarded project (2019 Bond, contract value $14.5M, awarded to Hellas Construction) — NOT an active bid. Scope included: (1) Manor New Tech High School — renovation of existing baseball and softball fields and dugouts with installation of synthetic turf (Major Play Matrix Helix with SBR crumb rubber infill); baseball field renovation completed 2023. (2) Manor Senior High School — ground-up construction of a new baseball/softball complex ('Mustang Ballpark') with dugouts, fencing, scoreboards, concession stands, restrooms, and lighting; completed 2024. No current open bid for turf work was found on Manor ISD's site as of this research; the 2025 bond ($385M, Nov 2025 ballot) covers building renovations, safety, and technology, with no athletic-field/turf line items identified. [Source: SRC-017 | Found at: https://www.manorisd.net/bond-program/bond-2019]
leads.Project_Address__c: Manor New Tech High School baseball/softball fields: 12904 Gregg Manor Road (baseball) / 12700 Gregg Manor Road (softball), Manor, TX 78653; Manor Senior High School Mustang Ballpark: 14832 FM 973, Manor, TX 78653

leads.Lead_Details__c: Elgin ISD 2024 Bond Athletic Complex project at 14000 County Line Road, Elgin, TX: new baseball turf field and new softball turf field, each with drainage, scoreboard, and fencing, plus new softball and baseball press boxes and 4 new tennis courts. Budget $6,800,000. Contractor: Tarkett Sports. Architect: Huckabee. Program/Project Manager: PROCEDEO. Opening projected Spring 2026. [Source: SRC-019 | Found at: https://www.elginisdbond.org/athleticcomplex]
leads.Project_Address__c: 14000 County Line Road, Elgin, TX 78621
leads.Title: Executive Director of Capital Projects

leads.Lead_Details__c: Bee Creek Sports Complex Phase II: construction of two additional synthetic multi-purpose sports fields (Fields 6 and 7) lined for soccer and lacrosse, plus associated infrastructure (seating with shade structures, sidewalks, fencing, field lighting, landscaping, additional parking). Funded by Travis County's 2023 parks bond (Proposition B, $276.44M). Budget for this phase approximately $6.95-8.35 million. As of April 2026 construction was about 20% complete. Sport confirmed as soccer (plus lacrosse, an excluded sport, but soccer field designation qualifies this lead); other sports at complex include baseball, softball, and football fields built in Phase One. [Source: SRC-024 | Found at: https://www.traviscountytx.gov/tnr/public-works/bee-creek-complex-outreach]
leads.Project_Address__c: 4440 Bee Creek Road, Spicewood/Lakeway, TX 78669
leads.Title: Purchasing Agent

leads.Lead_Details__c: Williamson County partnered with Lonestar Soccer Club to convert four natural grass soccer fields (Fields 6, 7, 8, and 9) to FIFA-certified synthetic turf at Southwest Williamson County Regional Park in Leander, plus resurfacing the adjacent 8-lane running track with artificial turf. Related solicitations: 25RFSQ12 (design/planning/landscape architecture/engineering SOQ, awarded to Halff) and 25RFP73 (Financial Sponsorship for Turf Fields, resulting in a sponsorship and use agreement with Lonestar Soccer Club of Austin, Inc.). Total project cost approximately $8.9-8.93 million: Williamson County funding conversion of one field via previously allocated Capital Improvement Program (CIP) funds ($2,425,020) plus ~$300,000 for the track, and Lonestar Soccer Club funding $6,500,000 for the other three fields. The fields support soccer and football activities (12,000+ annual games, practices, and training sessions). Construction bid proposals were due March 18, 2026; county broke ground on construction May 28, 2026. [Source: SRC-025, SRC-040 | Found at: https://www.wilcotx.gov/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=735 ; https://www.bidnetdirect.com/texas/williamsoncounty]
leads.Project_Address__c: 3005 CR 175, Leander, TX 78641 (Southwest Williamson County Regional Park)
leads.Title: Parks and Recreation Director

leads.Lead_Details__c: 2023 GO Bond (Proposition A: Parks, Recreation and Sports, $230M) - Old Settlers Park Multipurpose Complex Expansion. Existing 60-acre Multipurpose Complex (opened 2017) featured championship-level multipurpose fields with a mix of synthetic and natural grass turf; the 2023 bond funds six additional fields plus collegiate-level press boxes at the same complex. Groundbreaking for the broader Old Settlers Park buildout occurred September 30, 2024. Estimated 5-7 year completion window for full bond program. Existing complex fields are used for soccer/football and other multipurpose sports tourism events; turf material (synthetic vs. natural) for the six new fields not specified on public pages — needs validation. No specific bid/RFP number or point-of-contact listed on public procurement pages as of page review. [Source: SRC-031 | Found at: https://www.roundrocktexas.gov/city-departments/administration/2023-bond-proposed-project-details/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Old Settlers Park, 1301 Harrell Parkway, Round Rock, TX 78664

leads.Lead_Details__c: 2023 GO Bond - Old Settlers Park Athletic Performance Center: indoor gym, outdoor track, and four multipurpose fields, plus additional parking and relocation of Parks Department administrative offices. Part of the same Old Settlers Park buildout (groundbreaking held September 30, 2024) that also includes a 'track and field complex with several multi-use flat fields.' Specific sport designation (football/soccer/baseball/softball) for the four multipurpose fields is not stated on public pages, nor is turf type (synthetic vs. natural) — sport and turf specification needs validation. No bid/RFP number, contractor, or named point-of-contact found on public pages. [Source: SRC-031 | Found at: https://www.roundrocktexas.gov/city-departments/administration/2023-bond-proposed-project-details/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Old Settlers Park, 1301 Harrell Parkway, Round Rock, TX 78664

leads.Lead_Details__c: Bond-funded (2022 Bond Program, $5,594,835) project converting existing multi-use fields at Brushy Creek Sports Park to synthetic turf; facility includes 2 baseball/softball fields and 3 soccer/football fields. RFQ-10-05-223-CS-013 'Planning and Design Services: Brushy Creek Sports Park Synthetic Turf and Parking Improvements' (closed, published 11/10/2022, closed 12/16/2022) covered planning/design for turf and adjacent parking improvements. No named contact listed on page; general Parks & Recreation phone. [Source: SRC-032 | Found at: https://www.cedarparktexas.gov/bids.aspx?bidID=11]
leads.Project_Address__c: 2310 Brushy Creek Road, Cedar Park, TX 78613

leads.Lead_Details__c: Lakeline Park Phase 2, part of Bond Program 2022 (design phase funding $2,797,418); scope includes lighted artificial turf soccer fields and lighted softball fields, plus tennis/pickleball courts, sand volleyball, trails, play area and splash pads across an additional 30 acres. As of 2026, City Council approved a $2.1M design and engineering contract with SWA Group for Phase 2 improvements; Masterplan approved by Council April 23, 2026. Construction procurement/bid for the turf fields had not yet been posted as of this review — project currently in design phase. No named contact listed; general Parks & Recreation phone. [Source: SRC-032 | Found at: https://www.cedarparktexas.gov/1239/Bond-Program-2022]
leads.Project_Address__c: Lakeline Park, Cedar Park, TX 78613

leads.Lead_Details__c: Leander Municipal Athletic Complex (LMAC) at San Gabriel Park — 77-acre site being master-planned and designed by Parkhill. Scope includes six softball fields (girls) and multipurpose fields for soccer, lacrosse, cricket, and rugby; city guidance stated at least 50% of fields, if not more, may be synthetic turf or a combination of synthetic and natural turf. Phase 1 (~$10M) covers site development, utilities, infrastructure, parking, roadways, and a few multipurpose fields; construction bidding was anticipated spring 2024 with a targeted late summer/early-to-mid 2025 opening. Original RFQ was for planning, architectural, design and engineering services (awarded to Parkhill); a related construction bid/RFP may follow via the city's Bonfire procurement portal. [Source: SRC-033 | Found at: https://www.leandertx.gov/181/Proposition-No-2---Parks-Development]
leads.Project_Address__c: San Gabriel Park, northeast of the 183A Toll Bridge, north bank of the South San Gabriel River, Leander, TX (approx. 2401 US-183, Leander, TX)
leads.Projected_Start_Date__c: Spring 2024 (construction bidding); Phase 1 opening targeted late summer/early-to-mid 2025
leads.Title: Parks and Recreation Director

leads.Lead_Details__c: City of Kyle Sportsplex Master Plan (~$161.9M total, phased) on a ~80-90 acre site along FM 1626 near Plum Creek Meadows Drive. Outdoor plan includes a championship field, a youth soccer field, and multiple multi-use fields, plus per earlier concept plans 10 full-size turf fields and 5 youth turf fields, courts, trails, and a future 100,000 sq ft indoor recreation center. Area 1A (championship/youth fields, courts, playground) budgeted at $30.6M; Area 1B (multi-use fields, parking) at $11.2M. First phase funding of $33M identified ($14M from 2020 voter bonds, remainder from Tax Increment Reinvestment Zones). City Council raised concerns about a lack of dedicated baseball/softball fields in current concepts; Parks Director indicated additional designs addressing this are underway, so baseball/softball inclusion needs validation. Turf material/brand not specified in public materials reviewed. [Source: SRC-034 | Found at: https://www.cityofkyle.gov/city-hall/plans-and-studies/master-plans/]
leads.Project_Address__c: Sportsplex site along FM 1626 near Plum Creek Meadows Drive, Kyle, TX
leads.Title: Director of Parks and Recreation

failed_sources.reason: DemandStar bid detail pages (network.demandstar.com and www.demandstar.com) are login/JS-gated and returned no extractable content across multiple attempts. The one strong candidate identified (Williamson County Southwest Regional Park turf project, already captured via SRC-025/SRC-040) could not be traced to a citable DemandStar page URL, so no source-compliant lead could be produced from this source itself.

Subagent returned a candidate (City of Hutto 2024 Athletic Fields planning study) that names football/soccer/baseball/softball fields but explicitly describes them as natural grass with no artificial/synthetic turf specification. It fails the required turf-keyword match and was excluded from leads during consolidation; no other active turf procurement was found for this source.
